heading: Leases with Indian tribes
label: 1674 Leases with Indian tribes
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in subsec. (a), was in the original “this Act”, meaning , , , known as the Indian Health Care Improvement Act, which is classified principally to this chapter. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 94–43790 Stat. 1400section 1601 of this titleSept. 30, 1976

Amendments1988— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a) and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 100–7131980— inserted provision that property leased by the Secretary from an Indian tribe may be reconstructed or renovated by the Secretary pursuant to an agreement with such Indian tribe.Pub. L. 96–537

children.label: a
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary is authorized, in carrying out the purposes of this chapter, to enter into leases with Indian tribes for periods not in excess of twenty years. Property leased by the Secretary from an Indian tribe may be reconstructed or renovated by the Secretary pursuant to an agreement with such Indian tribe.
a beneficial interest in (where title is held by the United States in trust for the benefit of a tribe);
